required_patterns.version-management: Always check for 'latest' version via GitHub API or package manager, set fact for install version
required_patterns.binary-installation: Use get_url or apt for binary installation, verify with stat before downloading
required_patterns.service-lifecycle: Use systemd module to start/enable services after installation
required_patterns.idempotency-checks: Always check existence/status before installation or configuration changes
required_patterns.privilege-handling: Use become: no for user-specific operations (minikube, kubectl), become: yes for system operations

architectural_constraints.installation-flow: All infrastructure components follow: check existing -> fetch latest version -> download/install -> configure -> enable service
architectural_constraints.version-strategy: Support 'latest' as default with dynamic version fetching from GitHub releases or package repositories
architectural_constraints.user-context: Minikube and kubectl operations must run as non-root user, Docker and system packages require root
architectural_constraints.state-verification: Every installation task must include a status check command that can be used for verification

required_patterns.namespace-creation: Always create namespace with kubectl before deploying applications, use failed_when: false for idempotency
required_patterns.helm-repository-management: Add helm repos with loop, use register + failed_when: false for existing repos
required_patterns.helm-deployment: Use 'helm upgrade --install' pattern for idempotent deployments
required_patterns.manifest-based-deployment: For complex apps (Gogs, Sonarr, qBittorrent), use ansible.builtin.copy to create manifest files in /tmp, then kubectl apply
required_patterns.separated-task-files: Each application has its own task file in roles/helm_apps/tasks/{app}.yml
required_patterns.tailscale-ingress: All applications use Tailscale Ingress resources for external access, no LoadBalancer or NodePort services

architectural_constraints.deployment-method: Applications deployed via Helm charts OR in-line Kubernetes manifests (YAML in ansible.builtin.copy)
architectural_constraints.namespace-isolation: Each application or service group in separate namespace (git, media, pihole, tailscale)
architectural_constraints.ingress-strategy: All external access via Tailscale Ingress objects with custom hostnames (e.g., http://gogs, http://sonarr)
architectural_constraints.stateful-applications: StatefulSets with PersistentVolumeClaims for applications requiring persistent storage
architectural_constraints.configuration-management: Use ConfigMaps for application configuration, never hardcode values in manifests

required_patterns.variable-hierarchy: group_vars/all.yml for common variables, vault.yml for secrets, role defaults/main.yml for role-specific defaults
required_patterns.vault-integration: Sensitive values use {{ vault_variable_name | default('') }} pattern with empty string fallback
required_patterns.inventory-structure: hosts.yml defines hosts under 'all' group, with children groups for logical organization (kubernetes)
required_patterns.version-variables: All component versions defined as variables (minikube_version, kubectl_version, helm_version) with 'latest' as default
required_patterns.resource-variables: Kubernetes resource limits defined as variables (minikube_cpus, minikube_memory, minikube_disk_size)

architectural_constraints.secret-handling: All secrets must be defined in vault.yml (encrypted) and referenced with vault_ prefix in all.yml
architectural_constraints.defaults-vs-vars: Role defaults for component-specific configuration, group_vars for deployment-wide settings
architectural_constraints.no-inline-secrets: Never hardcode secrets, auth keys, or passwords - always use vault variables with defaults
architectural_constraints.conditional-execution: Check for variable existence and length before using sensitive variables (tailscale auth key pattern)

required_patterns.ssh-configuration: Backup SSH config before modifications with timestamp suffix
required_patterns.vault-encryption: All sensitive data encrypted with ansible-vault (vault.yml)
required_patterns.vpn-only-access: All services accessible only via Tailscale VPN, no public exposure
required_patterns.user-privilege-separation: Use become/become_user selectively, minimize root operations

architectural_constraints.no-public-exposure: No services exposed via public IPs or ports, all ingress via Tailscale
architectural_constraints.secret-encryption: Vault password file (.vault_pass) must exist for encrypted variable access
architectural_constraints.update-automation: Automatic security updates enabled via apt unattended-upgrades
architectural_constraints.template-based-config: System configuration files deployed via templates/ directory with .j2 extension
architectural_constraints.backup-before-modify: Always backup original configuration files before modifications

required_patterns.storage-class-verification: Always verify storage class exists before application deployment
required_patterns.default-storage-class: Use 'standard' storage class as default, patched with annotation
required_patterns.persistent-volumes: Use PersistentVolumeClaims in application manifests for stateful apps
required_patterns.volume-mounting: Minikube started with --mount-string for host path access
required_patterns.storage-provisioner-addon: Enable storage-provisioner addon if storage class missing

architectural_constraints.storage-backend: Minikube storage provisioner as only storage backend (no external storage)
architectural_constraints.volume-lifecycle: Volumes persist across pod restarts but are tied to Minikube cluster lifecycle
architectural_constraints.backup-strategy: Backup entire Minikube persistent volume directory, not individual volumes
architectural_constraints.storage-location: Storage provisioner uses Minikube's internal storage path

required_patterns.timestamp-naming: Backup files named with ISO8601 timestamp (minikube-volumes-backup-{timestamp}.tar.gz)
required_patterns.pre-backup-validation: Check source directory exists and is readable before backup
required_patterns.backup-metadata: Create info file with backup timestamp, source path, hostname, item count
required_patterns.error-handling: Use block/rescue pattern for backup tasks with proper error reporting
required_patterns.compression: Use tar.gz for compression with archive module

architectural_constraints.backup-scope: Full Minikube volume directory backup, not application-specific backups
architectural_constraints.transfer-method: Backup transfer via Tailscale network to remote hosts
architectural_constraints.retention-policy: No automated cleanup - manual retention management
architectural_constraints.restore-process: Manual restore process (extract to original location)

required_patterns.role-structure: Every role has tasks/main.yml, defaults/main.yml, meta/main.yml
required_patterns.task-organization: Complex roles split tasks into separate files included via include_tasks
required_patterns.role-dependencies: Dependencies declared in meta/main.yml (none currently used)
required_patterns.defaults-convention: All configurable values in defaults/main.yml with sensible defaults
required_patterns.idempotent-operations: All tasks designed to be safely re-run with changed_when and failed_when conditions

architectural_constraints.role-isolation: Each infrastructure component or application group as separate role
architectural_constraints.no-cross-dependencies: Roles do not depend on each other via meta dependencies (orchestrated via playbooks)
architectural_constraints.task-modularity: Application deployments split into per-app task files (gogs.yml, sonarr.yml, etc.)
architectural_constraints.template-usage: Only apt_updates role uses templates (for system config files)
architectural_constraints.standardized-naming: Role directories match component names (minikube, kubectl, helm, tailscale)

required_patterns.tag-based-execution: All role inclusions tagged for selective execution (infrastructure, applications, component-specific)
required_patterns.play-targeting: Use 'hosts: all' with specific role assignments
required_patterns.sequential-execution: Infrastructure before applications (controlled via import order)
required_patterns.privilege-escalation: become: yes at play level, overridden at task level when needed

architectural_constraints.execution-order: Must run infrastructure playbook before applications playbook
architectural_constraints.inventory-requirement: Must specify inventory with -i flag (no default in ansible.cfg points to file)
architectural_constraints.local-first-design: Default inventory uses ansible_connection: local for localhost
architectural_constraints.makefile-wrapper: Make targets provide simplified interface to complex ansible-playbook commands
architectural_constraints.single-host-focus: Designed for single target host (kimsufi), not multi-host orchestration
